prompt: A newly opened restaurant seen through its clean plate-glass window at noon on a weekday. Inside: all tables perfectly set with white tablecloths and cutlery, warm golden light from pendant lamps, two staff members standing near the counter with nothing to do, menus arranged on tables. Outside on the pavement: pedestrians walk past without entering. One person pauses briefly at the window, looks in, sees the empty tables, and continues walking. The restaurant is visibly ready, visibly good — but visibly empty. The street outside is busy; the restaurant is silent. Shot from across the street, eye level, 50mm lens, natural daylight, slight overexposure on the street exterior, warm interior light contrast. No text on menus, no readable signs, no writing anywhere visible.Negative: text, readable signs, labels, writing, menus with visible text, chalkboard writing, digital overlays, glowing elements, diagrams, charts
metaphor: A ready, well-prepared restaurant stands empty at lunch because no customers are present to attract other customers — circular dependency trapping a capable system.
